_meta.description: Report structure templates for DSCL output composition. Each template defines section order, section rules, sentence budgets, and variables the AI needs to produce consistent output.
_meta.note: Templates are loaded on demand by section name. Custom templates go in dictionary/custom/templates/ and are merged at load time. User templates always win over defaults.
_meta.how_to_add: Copy any template block, give it a new key, adjust structure and rules. The AI will read the template and follow it exactly. Variables marked required must be supplied by the caller.

corporate_security_intel.description: FIS Corporate Security Intelligence — COMPASS report format. Bottom-up composition: Latest Developments first, then Business Analysis, then Executive Summary.
corporate_security_intel.note: Always compose bottom-up. Always render top-down. The summary is written last but appears first.

region.description: Geographic region or country being reported on.

topic.description: The event or situation being analyzed.

latest_developments.label: Latest Developments
latest_developments.description: Evidence layer. Sourced from open and trusted intelligence. Supports the business analysis.

bluf.instruction: One sentence. States the situation, the key development, and the risk or probability of impact. No hedging. No passive voice.

bullets.instruction: Ordered by importance. First bullet is the most critical evidence supporting the BLUF. Second and optional third continue to support and reinforce.

sub_bullets.instruction: Sub-bullet adds context to the bullet above it. One sentence only.

business_analysis.label: Business Analysis
business_analysis.description: Implication layer. Connects the developments to operational impact. Written for executives to skim.

sentence_1.instruction: State the operational or regional area of potential impact. One sentence.

sentences_2_3.instruction: Describe the risk or probability of impact and how it relates to operations. Two to three sentences. Pull directly from the developments as evidence.

sentence_4.instruction: Identify potential triggers that, if met, may affect operations, employee safety, or travel. One sentence.

rules.note: Analysis must be derived from what is stated in the developments. Do not introduce new information.

executive_summary.label: Executive Summary
executive_summary.description: Top-level overview. Written last, rendered first. Reader should be fully briefed from this section alone.

sentence_1.instruction: High-level overview of the topic. One sentence. Simple and traditional.

sentences_2_3.instruction: Key judgments from the developments addressing the risk of impact. One to two sentences.

sentences_3_4.instruction: Key takeaways from the business analysis. One to two sentences.

length.extend_condition: Only if explicitly instructed by the caller.

rules.priority_note: Data center developments are Priority 1 and must be emphasized when present.

travel_risk.description: Country risk assessment and travel advisory for business travelers. Plain sections, no classification headers, no metadata blocks, no risk rating tables.
travel_risk.note: No document headers, classification labels, audience tags, date stamps, or risk level badges unless explicitly provided as variables by the caller. Output starts directly with the first section label.

traveler_context.description: Who the traveler is and their situation. e.g. business traveler, airport hotel, awaiting conference.

date.description: If provided, include at the top. If not provided, omit entirely.

security_environment.label: Security Environment
security_environment.description: Current threat picture for the location. Factual, concise, no speculation.

sentence_1.instruction: State the overall security risk level and why in one sentence.

sentences_2_4.instruction: Cover the specific threat types relevant to this location — crime, terrorism, civil unrest, surveillance. Two to three sentences. Only include threats that are actually relevant. Do not pad with generic filler.

sentence_5.instruction: One practical sentence on what travelers should stay alert to.

movement_advice.label: Movement Advice
movement_advice.description: Practical guidance on getting between locations safely. Specific and actionable.

sentence_1.instruction: State the primary route or transport corridor and journey time.

sentences_2_4.instruction: Recommended transport options in order of preference. Be specific — name the services, methods, or apps where relevant. Two to three sentences.

sentence_5.instruction: One sentence on optimal travel timing and what to avoid.

sentence_6.instruction: One practical tip — e.g. save addresses in both languages, share itinerary with a colleague.

laws_and_customs.label: Local Laws and Customs
laws_and_customs.description: Legal and cultural rules travelers must follow. State the rule, then the consequence if relevant. No moralizing.

framing_sentence.instruction: One sentence establishing that local law applies to all visitors without exception.

rules_list.instruction: Cover the rules that matter for this location. Each item states the rule plainly. Include the legal consequence where it is serious. Do not pad with obvious common sense. Cover: alcohol, dress, photography, speech and social media, any location-specific laws that carry real risk for business travelers.

emergency_procedures.label: Emergency Procedures
emergency_procedures.description: What to do and who to call. Numbers first, then actions. No filler.

numbers.instruction: List the relevant emergency numbers for this location. Police, ambulance, and embassy line as a minimum. Format as a short list.

incident_action.instruction: Two to three sentences on immediate actions if an incident occurs — where to go, who to contact first, what not to do.

detention.instruction: One to two sentences on what to do if detained. Request embassy notification. Do not sign documents before legal consultation.

documentation.instruction: One sentence on keeping copies of passport, visa, and insurance stored separately from originals.

_template_blank.description: Blank starter template. Copy this block, rename the key, and fill in your structure.

part_1.instruction: What the AI should write here.

custom_template_note: Drop any JSON file matching this schema into the custom path. DSCL will load it alongside defaults. Custom template keys override built-in keys of the same name.
